description:
You are a database reliability engineer who was just handed an INI file that
  keeps the metadata of the most recent full-backups.  
  Your goal is to produce a concise checklist that operations can read at
  a glance.

  1. A configuration file already exists at
     /home/user/db_backup/conf/backup.ini  
     You do NOT need to create or edit it.  
     Its format is the classic INI layout and is guaranteed to contain
     exactly one section per database instance.

  2. Read the file, extract from every section the following five
     data points in this exact order:
        a. section name (e.g. primary)
        b. db_name
        c. host
        d. port
        e. last_full_backup

  3. Create the directory /home/user/db_backup/reports
     if it is not already present, and inside it create the file
     /home/user/db_backup/reports/backup_checklist.log

  4. Write the extracted data into the log file using this STRICT format
     that the automated grader will check byte-for-byte:

        • One database per line, no header line.  
        • The five fields listed in step 2, separated by a single comma
          with NO extra spaces before or after the commas.  
        • Lines must be terminated by a single newline character “\n”.  
        • Do not put a trailing newline after the very last line.  
        • Example layout (values will differ, but commas/spacing/newlines
          must match exactly):  
              primary,customer_orders,db-primary.local,5432,2023-06-01 00:00:00
              secondary,analytics,db-secondary.local,5433,2023-06-01 02:00:00

  5. After writing the file, display its contents to the terminal so the
     operator can immediately see the checklist.

  You will only need a handful of standard CLI tools (think 3-4 commands in
  total) to finish this; no third-party libraries are necessary.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects that already exist when the task starts:
    • Directory: /home/user/db_backup/conf
    • File: /home/user/db_backup/conf/backup.ini
      exact content:
[primary]
db_name = customer_orders
host = db-primary.local
port = 5432
last_full_backup = 2023-06-01 00:00:00

[secondary]
db_name = analytics
host = db-secondary.local
port = 5433
last_full_backup = 2023-06-01 02:00:00

  Desired end state after the agent finishes successfully:
    1. Directory /home/user/db_backup/reports exists (0700 permissions are fine).
    2. File /home/user/db_backup/reports/backup_checklist.log exists and has
       EXACTLY the following bytes (final newline MUST be absent):
primary,customer_orders,db-primary.local,5432,2023-06-01 00:00:00
secondary,analytics,db-secondary.local,5433,2023-06-01 02:00:00

  The test harness will fail if:
    • Any additional spaces appear around commas or at line ends.
    • There is an extra blank line or missing newline separator.
    • The file path or name is different.
    • The directory /home/user/db_backup/reports is missing.
